title: We need to carefully compare all competitors in all scopes with out project, and make sure we have the richest features set
body:
We need to make sure our engine works as readonly or mutable as per user configuration, CST/AST for all popular programming languages, data exchange formats, and other popular formal languages, and also natural languages (including actual parsing of their grammar with no semantic checks, meaning matching and logic correctness is out of the scope of this project, but grammatical correctness, syntax correctness and so on - should be fully supported).

We also should have advanced shared concepts space between languages, which should be reused when only matched exactly, so it will help to simplify automated translation between any languages, transformation and mutation of texts/code/messages in any language and so on.

So we need to be able to fully support storage (presenting as links notation (text based as in https://github.com/link-foundation/links-notation), binary links as in https://github.com/linksplatform/doublets-rs and https://github.com/linksplatform/doublets-web), and also Rust traits/types representation.

github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai will be our the most heavy user, so we need to make sure any language can be treated easily as data, and we can do any transformations and substitutions in style of github.com/link-foundation/link-cli, and also other traditional API adapters, like chaining, direct OOP methods and so on. So all the same operations should be possible to be used by all kinds of ways, so user can decide whatever he prefer best and use it. All of APIs styles should support all the same operations up on the languages described in meta-language.

Our meta language should also provide ability to translate any other language to meta language and from meta language. Meaning all data manipulations should be done up on our meta language, but if we want to keep working with single language for example for javascript to javascript transformation - we just restrict ourselves with using only features of meta language that JavaScript supports exactly and so on.

If we do cross language translation, we need to make sure our API is expandable and all missing translation from meta language and to meta language are expandable and configurable by end user, so he will be able to support cross language rules based translation, with full freedom of configuration, so everything in our system should be replaceable, configurable and so on. So widest possible range of operations for any language via the prism of meta language will be supported.

We should check that nothing is defered or left unimplemented in our vision and roadmap. We should have 100% tests coverage, which should copy most of test cases from our competitors in each sector/scope.

For each aspect of this task, we should create issues on GitHub, with clear blocked by markings, so we know in which order to execute all the task, as we the result we will combine all tasks in pull request for this specific task.

So all tasks should also be subtasks of this task. All tasks will be executed on the branch for this task, so everything will be merged into single big pull request.

If it is required it is ok to make architectural and breaking changes. Our goal is to have the best possible tool for exactly any possible task with languages.

We need to collect data related about the issue to this repository, make sure we compile that data to `./docs/case-studies/issue-{id}` folder, and use it to do deep case study analysis (also make sure to search online for additional facts and data), list of each and all requirements from the issue, and propose possible solutions and solution plans for each requirement (we should also check known existing components/libraries, that solve similar problem or can help in solutions).
